steps.title: List the eliminated players
steps.content: In round 1, half of 240 players are eliminated, so $p=120$. Then the eliminated counts keep halving: round 2 has 60, round 3 has 30, and round 4 has 15. This is exponential decay with starting value $240$ and multiplier $\frac{1}{2}$.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content: The eliminated count halves each round, so the multiplier is $0.5=\frac{1}{2}$. A model with round number $r$ is $$p=240\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^r.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: $p=240{ \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}^{r}$ has starting value $240$ and halving factor $\frac{1}{2}$. $p=15{ \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}^{r}$ starts with $15$ instead of $240$. Two other options use a factor of $2$, which grows each round instead of shrinking. $p=240{ \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}^{r}$ is correct.
